display_title: God and Human Beings in the Technologically-Mediated Living Condition: Philip K. Dick's Literary Theology in the VALIS Trilogy
author: Laurie Jui-hua Tseng
card_summary: An academic article exploring Philip K. Dick's literary theology in the VALIS trilogy, arguing that Dick portrays the technologically-mediated human condition as a simulacrum of God's divine matrix. Examines the concepts of God as Zebra, the distinction between divine and technological systems through love and living presence, and Dick's concealment of his Gnostic leanings.

filename: [Ace Double D-193 ] Dick, Philip K - The Man Who Japed (2013) - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: The Man Who Japed (2013 Ebook Edition)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A later ebook edition of Dick's 1956 satirical novel set in a future society governed by rigid moral conformity enforced by the Moral Reclamation movement. Allen Purcell, a respectable propagandist, discovers he has unconsciously vandalized a statue of the movement's founder, setting off a chain of events that challenges the social order.

filename: [Alas v30 ] Dick, Philip Kindred - Philip K Dick is Dead - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Philip K. Dick is Dead, Alas
author: Michael Bishop
card_summary: A satirical alternate history novel by Michael Bishop set in a world where America won the Vietnam War and Richard Nixon was reelected four times. Philip K. Dick is remembered for realist novels while his SF circulates illegally in samizdat form. When a bearded amnesiac appears in a Georgia therapist's office, the boundaries between Dick's fiction and reality blur in a tribute to Dick's own literary preoccupations.

filename: [Five Star Paperback] Erik Davis - TechGnosis_ Myth, Magic & Mysticism in the Age of Information (2005, Five Star) - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: TechGnosis: Myth, Magic & Mysticism in the Age of Information
author: Erik Davis
card_summary: A wide-ranging cultural study exploring the mystical and mythological impulses underlying the development of information technology, from the printing press through radio, television, and the internet. Davis traces how Gnostic, hermetic, and magical traditions have persistently shaped humanity's relationship with technology, with significant attention to science fiction authors including Philip K. Dick.

filename: [Fortnight 1999-nov iss. 381] Techgnosis_ Myth, Magic and Mysticism in the Age of Informationby Erik Davis{Review by_ Brendan Duffin}(1999 November)[10.2307_25559785]{54386821} libgen.li.pdf
author: Brendan Duffin
card_summary: A brief book review of Erik Davis's TechGnosis published in the Irish magazine Fortnight in November 1999. Reviews Davis's exploration of the intersection between mysticism, myth, and information technology from the printing press to the internet age.

filename: [Leonardo vol. 33 iss. 4] Techgnosis_ Myth, Magic + Mysticism in the Age of Informationby Erik Davis{Review by_ Kevin Murray}(2000)[10.2307_1576923]{34008202} libgen.li.pdf
author: Kevin Murray
card_summary: A review of Erik Davis's TechGnosis published in Leonardo, the MIT Press journal of art, science, and technology. Evaluates Davis's argument about the persistent entanglement of mystical and magical thinking with the development of information technologies.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 2001-mar vol. 28 iss. 1] Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick{Lorenzo DiTommaso}(2001 March)[10.2307_4240950]{33300601} libgen.li (1).pdf
display_title: Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick (duplicate copy)
author: Lorenzo DiTommaso
card_summary: Duplicate copy of DiTommaso's article on Gnosticism and dualism in Dick's early fiction.

filename: [Science Fiction Studies 2001-mar vol. 28 iss. 1] Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick{Lorenzo DiTommaso}(2001 March)[10.2307_4240950]{33300601} libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick
author: Lorenzo DiTommaso
card_summary: A scholarly article from Science Fiction Studies examining the Gnostic and dualistic themes in Philip K. Dick's early fiction. DiTommaso traces Dick's engagement with Gnostic cosmology and the persistent dualism between authentic and counterfeit reality that would become central to his mature work.

filename: [The Journal of Religion 2013-jan vol. 93 iss. 1] Jeffrey J Kripal,Mutants and Mystics_ Science Fiction, Superhero Comics, and the Paranormal{Review by_ William Elison}(2013 January)[10.1086_669849]{33181244} libgen.l.pdf
display_title: Review: Mutants and Mystics by Jeffrey J. Kripal (Journal of Religion)
author: William Elison
card_summary: A scholarly book review from The Journal of Religion evaluating Jeffrey J. Kripal's Mutants and Mystics, which examines the connections between science fiction, superhero comics, and paranormal experience. The review assesses Kripal's argument that popular culture genres serve as vehicles for genuine mystical and paranormal encounters.

display_title: Gnostic Awakenings: Science Fiction, Myth-Making, and the Religious Imagination
author: Daniel Atik
card_summary: A Wesleyan University honors thesis in Religion exploring the intersection of science fiction, myth-making, and the religious imagination. Examines how science fiction authors, including Philip K. Dick, engage with Gnostic themes and religious meaning-making through speculative storytelling.

filename: Kripal, Jeffrey J - Authors of the impossible _ the paranormal and the sacred (2011,2010, The University of Chicago Press) - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Authors of the Impossible: The Paranormal and the Sacred
author: Jeffrey J. Kripal
card_summary: A study by Rice University religious studies professor Jeffrey Kripal arguing that paranormal phenomena deserve serious intellectual engagement. Drawing on cases from psychical research, UFO encounters, and science fiction, Kripal proposes that the paranormal represents a form of the sacred that challenges conventional categories of both science and religion.

filename: Kripal, Jeffrey J - Mutants & mystics_ science fiction, superhero comics, and the paranormal (2011_2015, University of Chicago Press) - libgen.li.pdf
display_title: Mutants & Mystics: Science Fiction, Superhero Comics, and the Paranormal
author: Jeffrey J. Kripal
card_summary: Jeffrey Kripal examines the deep connections between paranormal experience and the creation of science fiction and superhero comics. Through case studies of authors and artists including Philip K. Dick, Kripal argues that popular culture genres encode genuine encounters with the extraordinary, functioning as modern forms of mythological and mystical expression.

display_title: Pink Light and Iron: Epistemo-Critical Writing in Walter Benjamin and Philip K. Dick
author: John McLoughlin
card_summary: A Cardiff University doctoral thesis comparing the labyrinthine, non-linear approaches to philosophical representation in Walter Benjamin's Arcades Project and The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick. Argues that both works embody a pluralistic notion of truth that can only be glimpsed indirectly, exploring what McLoughlin calls epistemo-critical writing.

display_title: Ontological Instability and the Internal Author in the Fiction of Philip K. Dick
author: Ciarán Kavanagh
card_summary: A Master of Arts thesis from the National University of Ireland, Cork, examining ontological instability and the concept of the internal author in Philip K. Dick's fiction. Explores how Dick's narratives destabilize the reader's sense of reality and embed authorial self-reflection within the fictional world.

filename: Space Opera_ The Aesthetics of Personhood in the Works and Worlds.pdf
display_title: Space Opera: The Aesthetics of Personhood in the Works and Worlds of Philip K. Dick
author: Gabriel Francis Mamola
card_summary: A doctoral dissertation from the University of Texas at Arlington examining the aesthetics of personhood in the works and worlds of Philip K. Dick. Analyzes how Dick's fiction constructs, interrogates, and deconstructs the concept of personhood through the lens of science fiction's formal and thematic possibilities.
